reasoning: The market price is currently 6.5%. While there is upside tail risk to inflation in 2025 due to proposed tariffs by the incoming Trump administration (which could add 1-1.5% to the price level) and potentially loose financial conditions, there is a powerful disinflationary force baked into 2025: shelter inflation. Because of the known lag in BLS shelter data, cooling real-time market rents from 2023-2024 will heavily pull down the housing component of CPI in 2025, potentially shaving up to 1% off headline CPI. To reach a 4.1% year-over-year increase in any month, goods and energy prices would have to spike dramatically enough to not only offset the shelter disinflation but also drive the headline number well above current levels (~2.6%). While the second half of 2025 faces unfavorable base effects (replacing low MoM prints from H2 2024), the high bar of 4.1% makes it unlikely without concurrently surging energy prices and a swift, comprehensive implementation of severe tariffs without exceptions. I rate the probability slightly above the market at 9% to account for geopolitical and tariff execution tail risks.
